### SEO Description

**Your stomach acid production drops nearly 40% after age 60 — and that silent decline could be making your medications stop working.** Dr. Victor Kane, vascular surgeon, explains the gastric pH mechanism behind medication absorption failure and reveals which 5 drug classes are most affected (including thyroid medication and bone-protecting drugs).

**What this video covers:**
- Why "my medication stopped working" is a real physiological phenomenon after 60, not patient imagination
- The 5 drug classes most vulnerable to age-related pH changes (with specific absorption mechanisms)
- The PPI cascade: how acid-reducing medications create a compounding absorption problem
- A practical protocol for discussing medication bioavailability with your doctor
- The liquid/soft-chew alternatives that bypass the gastric acid requirement entirely

In this video, you'll learn:
• Why levothyroxine absorption can drop by 30% in seniors with low stomach acid — and what the Thyroid (2018) study found
• How calcium carbonate switches from absorbed to unabsorbed as pH rises above 3.0
• The PPI paradox: taking acid-blocking meds that make your other meds less effective
• Which medications have acid-independent alternatives your doctor can prescribe
• What to actually say to your physician — a word-for-word conversation guide

Unlike medication-warning videos that just tell you what to fear, Dr. Kane explains the *mechanisms* — so you can have an informed conversation with your doctor rather than panicking. Every claim is backed by peer-reviewed studies.

📌 **TIMESTAMPS:**
0:00 — The Medical Mystery: When Medications Stop Working
2:15 — Your Stomach at 30 vs. Your Stomach at 65: The pH Shift
4:30 — Drug Class #1: Thyroid Medications (Levothyroxine Absorption Failure)
6:10 — Drug Class #2 & 3: Calcium + Bone-Protecting Drugs
7:45 — Drug Class #4 & 5: Antifungals + Blood Thinners
9:20 — The PPI Cascade: When the "Solution" Makes It Worse
11:05 — The Practical Protocol: What to Ask Your Doctor
13:30 — The End Screen: What Your Fingertips Are Telling You

🔬 **SOURCES:**
• Thyroid (2018) — Levothyroxine absorption in low-acid conditions
• NEJM (Recker) — Calcium carbonate bioavailability and gastric pH
• Endocrine (2019) — Liquid T4 formulation vs. tablet absorption

⚠️ **DISCLAIMER:** This video is for educational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Do not stop or change any medication without consulting your physician. Discuss medication bioavailability with your healthcare provider.

## VID-020: "Your Iron Levels Look Normal After 60 — But Your Cells Are Starving"

**Core Insight:** Ferritin can appear normal despite functional iron deficiency — the "Ferritin Mask" framework. sTfR testing is the missing diagnostic.
**Cross-link Target:** VID-021 (Fingertip Tingling)
**Upload Target:** Friday, June 6, 10:00 AM EST

---

✅ **Recommended Title:** #1 — "Your Iron Levels Look Normal After 60 — But Your Cells Are Starving"
**Rationale:** The "Normal But Suffering" pattern (P015) was added to the channel's library specifically for this video, making it a validated formula. The emotional contrast between "looks normal" and "cells are starving" creates immediate curiosity and personal relevance — every senior who's been told their iron is "fine" despite fatigue will click. ---

### SEO Description

**Your iron levels "look normal" on your blood test — but your cells might still be starving for oxygen. Here's the hidden reason why standard iron tests miss functional iron deficiency after age 60.** Dr. Victor Kane, vascular surgeon, explains the ferritin deception, reveals the one blood test that catches what others miss, and gives you a word-for-word script for requesting it from your doctor.

**What this video covers:**
- Why ferritin is the *worst* single marker for iron status after 60 — and what it actually measures (iron storage vs. iron delivery)
- The one blood test (sTfR) that reveals what ferritin hides — and why most doctors don't order it
- Three hidden "iron drains" that accelerate after 60 — even when supplementation is adequate
- A word-for-word conversation script for requesting sTfR testing from your physician
- Why supplementing iron without evaluating the GI tract first can be dangerous

In this video, you'll learn:
• How ferritin acts as an acute-phase reactant — and why inflammation makes it *look* normal even when tissue iron is critically low
• The Lancet Haematology (2019) meta-analysis confirming sTfR's superiority for detecting functional iron deficiency
• The 3 iron drains after 60: GI microbleeding, medication interference, and reduced gastric acid conversion
• Why 22% of seniors with "normal" iron studies show functional deficiency on sTfR testing (J Nutr Health Aging 2023)
• The exact conversation script: "Dr. Kane recommends I request a serum transferrin receptor test — can we add that to my labs?"

This isn't another "take more iron" video. Dr. Kane explains the *diagnostic gap* so you can advocate for the right testing — because you can't fix what your doctor can't see.

📌 **TIMESTAMPS:**
0:00 — "Your Iron is Normal" — Why That Might Not Be True
2:45 — Ferritin vs. Iron Delivery: What the Standard Test Actually Measures
5:00 — The sTfR Test: The One Marker That Doesn't Lie
6:30 — The 3 Iron Drains After 60 (Why Supplementation Alone Fails)
8:45 — The CV Risk Connection: Iron Deficiency and Heart Risk (BMJ Open 2024)
10:15 — Your Word-for-Word Doctor Script
12:00 — Next: Your Fingertips Hold Another Secret

🔬 **SOURCES:**
• Lancet Haematology (2019) — sTfR meta-analysis for functional iron deficiency
• J Nutr Health Aging (2023) — 22% undiagnosed rate in seniors
• BMJ Open (2024) — Iron deficiency cardiovascular risk in older adults

⚠️ **DISCLAIMER:** This video is for educational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Do not begin iron supplementation without physician guidance — excess iron can cause organ damage. Always discuss testing and treatment with your healthcare provider.
